title: GDPR DPIA Remediation Monitoring and Mitigation Closure

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that GDPR Art.35(7)(d) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.1.35: GDPR DPIA Remediation Monitoring and Mitigation Closure.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.1.35' running on gdpr_dpia_mitigation_register.csv, index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sc_task", archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk Add-on for GitHub (Splunkbase 5596)
spl:
| inputlookup gdpr_dpia_mitigation_register.csv
| rename mitigation_task as task_number
| join task_number [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sc_task" earliest=-180d
    | eval done=if(state IN ("Closed","Resolved"),1,0)
    | stats latest(state) as state latest(_time) as last_update max(done) as closed by number
    | rename number as task_number
]
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(due_date,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where closed=0 AND age_days > 0
| table processing_activity, mitigation_description, task_number, state, due_date, age_days
| sort - age_days
description: The DPIA must include measures to address risks. This use case ties DPIA mitigation items to ITSM work orders, tracks age and overdue status, and verifies technical completion signals (e.g., control deployment logs) where available.
value: The DPIA must include measures to address risks. This use case ties DPIA mitigation items to ITSM work orders, tracks age and overdue status, and verifies technical completion signals (e.g., control deployment logs) where available.
implementation: (1) Export mitigation action list from each approved DPIA into `gdpr_dpia_mitigation_register.csv`; (2) create linked ServiceNow tasks with due dates; (3) alert owners weekly on overdue items; (4) optionally confirm technical controls via GitHub merge events or Terraform apply logs; (5) close DPIA only when all mitigations are verified.
visualization: Table (overdue mitigations), Single value (open high-risk mitigations), Burndown chart (open vs closed), Bar chart (age by owner).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Ticket_Management.All_Ticket_Management by All_Ticket_Management.status, All_Ticket_Management.priority, All_Ticket_Management.category | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: The DPIA must include measures to address risks — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk Add-on for GitHub (Splunkbase 5596).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `gdpr_dpia_mitigation_register.csv`, `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:sc_task"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Export mitigation action list from each approved DPIA into `gdpr_dpia_mitigation_register.csv`; (2) create linked ServiceNow tasks with due dates; (3) alert owners weekly on overdue items; (4) optionally confirm technical controls via GitHub merge events or Terraform apply logs; (5) close DPIA only when all mitigations are verified.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup gdpr_dpia_mitigation_register.csv
| rename mitigation_task as task_number
| join task_number [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sc_task" earliest=-180d
    | eval done=if(state IN ("Closed","Resolved"),1,0)
    | stats latest(state) as state latest(_time) as last_update max(done) as closed by number
    | rename number as task_number
]
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(due_date,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where closed=0 AND age_days > 0
| table processing_activity, mitigation_description, task_number, state, due_date, age_days
| sort - age_days
```

Understanding this SPL

**GDPR DPIA Remediation Monitoring and Mitigation Closure (Art. 35(7)(d))** — The DPIA must include measures to address risks. This use case ties DPIA mitigation items to ITSM work orders, tracks age and overdue status, and verifies technical completion signals (e.g., control deployment logs) where available.

Documented **Data sources**: `gdpr_dpia_mitigation_register.csv`, `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:sc_task"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk Add-on for GitHub (Splunkbase 5596). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Renames fields with `rename` for clarity or joins.
• Joins to a subsearch with `join` — set `max=` to match cardinality and avoid silent truncation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where closed=0 AND age_days > 0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **GDPR DPIA Remediation Monitoring and Mitigation Closure (Art. 35(7)(d))**): table processing_activity, mitigation_description, task_number, state, due_date, age_days
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Ticket_Management.All_Ticket_Management by All_Ticket_Management.status, All_Ticket_Management.priority, All_Ticket_Management.category | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**GDPR DPIA Remediation Monitoring and Mitigation Closure (Art. 35(7)(d))** — The DPIA must include measures to address risks. This use case ties DPIA mitigation items to ITSM work orders, tracks age and overdue status, and verifies technical completion signals (e.g., control deployment logs) where available.

Documented **Data sources**: `gdpr_dpia_mitigation_register.csv`, `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:sc_task"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk Add-on for GitHub (Splunkbase 5596). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Ticket_Management.All_Ticket_Management` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (overdue mitigations), Single value (open high-risk mitigations), Burndown chart (open vs closed), Bar chart (age by owner).
